Pyrus communis is a deciduous tree or shrub species in the family Rosaceae, known for its edible fruit, the wild pear. 

The health benefits of Pyrus communis include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. 

The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus communis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
